text: Providing uninterrupted
support.
demonstrating agility and dedication to duty.

Since the onset of the pandemic, our business teams across the Group have responded swiftly and proactively to help alleviate the impact on communities while ensuring the safety of our employees. I am proud of the wide range of measures we have implemented,

including financial support, provision of medical supplies, free coverage, access to healthcare resources, streamlined service processes and expedited claims payments. True to our commitment to finding every reason to pay,
we paid over US$16 billion in insurance benefits and claims during the year, providing vital financial protection to our customers.
Benefits and Claims
Despite VONB declining to US$2.765 billion due to measures to contain the spread of COVID-19, sales momentum turned positive as restrictions eased,
and 2021 began with very strong momentum, with VONB growing 15% year-on-year in the first two months. High-quality and recurring
earnings from our large and growing in-force book drove a 5% increase in OPAT in 2020, while UFSG grew 7% during the period. As at 31 December 2020,
EV Equity reached a record high of US$67.2 billion, and the Group LCSM cover ratio stood at 374%, demonstrating
demonstrating strong capital and rigorous financial management.
The Board has proposed a 7.5% increase in the 2020 final dividend to 100.30 Hong Kong cents per share. This is in line with AIA's long-standing prudent, sustainable and progressive dividend
policy to capture growth opportunities while maintaining financial flexibility.
AIA has a number of significant advantages that set us apart in highly competitive markets. We have an unparalleled platform in Asia, built over decades of operating in the region,

and hold leading positions in most of the markets where we operate. Our unwavering 100 per cent focus on Asia enables us to fully capture growth opportunities in the world's most attractive life insurance region.
This focus, combined with our leadership team's extensive experience in Asia and the outstanding quality of our people,
key driver of our performance

Our unparalleled distribution strength enables us to reach customers directly and provide high-quality professional advice to help meet the substantial and growing long-term protection and savings needs of Asian consumers.
Today, people expect more than ever that companies address their needs in a manner consistent with long-term sustainability. AIA has a market-leading brand,
built on the foundation of helping people live Healthier, Longer, Better Lives.

We aim to make AIA a global leader in technology application, enhancing connectivity, expanding scale and improving efficiency. Deeper insights from data analytics enable us to create unique and personalised customer experiences.
Products and services that are relevant and reflect individual needs and lifestyles are transforming the way people view life insurance.
building lasting customer relationships.
Despite 2020 being an unprecedented year, I am more optimistic than ever about the prospects for the Asian life insurance industry and the future of AIA. We continue to challenge
ourselves and evolve our priorities to ensure we remain appropriately positioned to meet the changing expectations and financial needs of our customers. Our new corporate
strategy builds on AIA's existing strengths and advances our transformation to best serve our customers in the future.
2020 Annual Report 011

The primary impact of the pandemic on our 2020 financial performance was on new business sales, as containment measures in many of our markets restricted face-to-face sales activities, particularly in the first half of 2020. Although VONB of US$2.765 billion in 2020 decreased by 33% compared to 2019,

we rapidly adopted new digital tools, and the widespread easing of movement restrictions supported a recovery in sales momentum. AIA China made the largest contribution to the Group's VONB in 2020 and was our first reporting segment to show a recovery in sales momentum, as the region progressively eased strict lockdowns from March 2020 onwards.

Excluding sales to Mainland Chinese visitors to Hong Kong, which remained at low levels, VONB in the rest of the Group's segments rebounded strongly in the second half, growing 23% compared to the first half.
contributed the most to the Group's VONB and was our first reporting segment to show signs of recovering sales momentum as the region progressively eased strict lockdowns from March 2020.
Excluding sales to Mainland Chinese visitors which remained low, VONB in the rest of the Group's segments rebounded strongly in the second half,
growing 23% compared to the first half. The Group's new business momentum continued to be strong in 2021, with VONB in the
first two months 15% higher than the same period in 2020.
Embedded value (EV) equity as at 31 December 2020 reached a record high of US$67.185 billion, up US$5.277 billion before dividends of US$1.997 billion,
This was driven by EV operating profit of US$7.243 billion, which offset the impact of lower government bond yields. EV operating profit included US$549 million from positive operating variances,
reflecting our continued positive overall experience relative to our EV assumptions.
Our high-quality, recurring earnings sources and active management of our in-force portfolio drove OPAT growth of 5% to US$5.942 billion and maintained a stable operating margin of 16.9%.

Renewal premiums received increased by 10%, with total recurring premiums accounting for over 90% of premiums received in 2020. OPAT growth benefited from significantly positive claims experience throughout the year,
which offset the impact of lower yields on new fixed income investments and the assumed reduction in long-term equity investment returns. As expected,
the exceptionally positive medical claims experience reported in the first half did not recur in the second half, but we continued
to see a positive contribution from this source to OPAT growth. Persistency improved in the second half as lapse experience at AIA Thailand returned to normal,
with the Group's persistency at 95%.
UFSG of US$5.843 billion increased by 7%, driven by the continued growth of our in-force portfolio and our active management of it, which was sufficient
to offset the impact of lower expected investment returns. As at 31 December 2020, the Group's financial position remained very strong, with Free
Surplus of US$13.473 billion after payment of dividends of US$1.997 billion.
As at 31 December 2020, the solvency ratio of our principal operating company, AIA Company Limited (AIA Co.), remained very strong at 489%. As at 31 December 2020,
under the new group regulatory framework, the Group LCSM cover ratio was 374%. Going forward, we will disclose the Group LCSM
cover ratio as the primary measure of the Group's regulatory solvency.

The Board has proposed a final dividend of 100.30 Hong Kong cents per share, an increase of 7.5%, subject to shareholders' approval at the Company's forthcoming Annual General Meeting. This brings the total dividend for 2020 to 135.30 Hong Kong cents per share,
an increase of 6.9% over the total dividend for 2019. This is in line with AIA's long-standing prudent, sustainable and progressive dividend policy, which
allows the Group to capture future growth opportunities while maintaining financial flexibility.

We remain confident in the business opportunities for AIA across the region. We will continue to focus on investing capital to deliver profitable new business growth and leverage our competitive advantages,
while maintaining prudent financial management and delivering long-term shareholder value.

Although VONB of US$2.765 billion in 2020 decreased by 33% compared with 2019, we rapidly adopted new digital tools and the widespread easing of mobility restrictions supported a recovery in sales momentum.
AIA China was the largest contributor to the Group's VONB in 2020 and was our first reporting segment to show a recovery in sales momentum, as the
region progressively eased strict lockdowns from March 2020. Excluding Mainland Chinese visitor sales to Hong Kong, which remained low, VONB
in the rest of the Group rebounded strongly in the second half of the year, growing by 23% compared with the first half. The Group's new business momentum continued to be strong in 2021,
with VONB in the first two months 15% higher than the same period in 2020.
AIA China recorded successful momentum in 2021, with VONB in the first two months showing very strong growth compared to the same period in 2020.
VONB of US$968 million in 2020 decreased by 17% from 2019, primarily due to limited sales during the initial outbreak of COVID-19 in the first quarter. As movement restrictions eased,
new business momentum improved rapidly. VONB resumed our customary seasonal pattern, with greater weighting towards the first half of the year.
In 2020, AIA China was the largest contributor to the Group's VONB.

Following the implementation of travel restrictions, new business sales from Mainland Chinese visitors to AIA Hong Kong have been effectively suspended since early February 2020, resulting in a significant decline in VONB.
The VONB margin recorded a decrease, reflecting the impact of underwriting expense overruns due to reduced new business volumes, changes in product mix and lower interest rates.
Macau resumed the Individual Visit Scheme for Mainland Chinese travelers at the end of September 2020. This supported a recovery in sales to Mainland Chinese visitors to Macau,
which accounted for over one-third of total ANP at AIA Macau in the fourth quarter.
018 AIA Group Limited
